topics.name: CommonTechStacks
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about common tech stacks used on projects worldwide.

topics.name: AssemblingaTechStack
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to put together a tech stack that balances the needs of an application and the team building it.

topics.name: TechStackRamping
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about techniques that enable you to quickly ramp up on new tech stacks and start shipping new features.

topics.name: LivingDocumentation
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to create helpful documentation that evolves along with the system it describes.

topics.name: RequirementsGathering
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to gather requirements for features, bugs, and other tasks so that you know what to build.

topics.name: TestingStrategies
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about test-driven development, the testing pyramid, and how to apply unit, integration, and end-to-end testing when creating software.

topics.name: TestPatterns
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about common test patterns such as the four-phase test, inside-out testing, and other practical techniques to make writing automated tests more efficient and enjoyable.

topics.name: TestDoubles
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about test doubles (stubs, spies, and mocks), how they can be used in your project, and when to use them in your tests.

topics.name: TestSmellsandFlakiness
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about common anti-patterns when writing automated tests and some techniques to help your test suite run soundly every time.

topics.name: CodeQuality
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn why the quality of your code is important and how it affects the safety and reliability of your codebase.

topics.name: CodeSmells
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about signals in your codebase that can indicate poor design or the lack of best practices.

topics.name: FourRulesofSimpleDesign
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about writing simple code that is tested, singular, and easy to understand.

topics.name: DesignPatterns
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about typical solutions to common problems in software design.

topics.name: RefactoringTechniques
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about common ways to better restructure existing code without changing its underlying behavior.

topics.name: HistoryofComputing
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the history of computing and some of the most important events that shaped the Information Age.

topics.name: Hardware
topics.description: With this topic, you will learn about the essential computer components and the different roles they play.

topics.name: OperatingSystems
topics.description: With this topic, you will learn about the underlying software that coordinates the different components of a computer.

topics.name: Runtimes
topics.description: With this topic, you will learn about the process of turning human-readable code into commands that the computer can understand.

topics.name: SpectrumofPlatforms
topics.description: With this topic, you will learn about the different combinations of hardware, operating systems, and runtimes used to run software applications.

topics.name: HistoryofCraftsmanship
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about how craftsmanship has been applied across different trades, cultures, and periods in human history.

topics.name: PrinciplesofModernCraftsmanship
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the principles of modern craftsmanship and how they can be utilized in any occupation.

topics.name: TheSoftwareCraftsmanshipMovement
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the software craftsmanship movement and how it can be applicable far into the future.

topics.name: LearningasanApprentice
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn patterns and techniques to learn new topics and effectively build skills as an apprentice.

topics.name: CoachingasaMentor
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about patterns and techniques to mentor an apprentice in their journey to graduation effectively.

topics.name: TypesofData
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the spectrum of unstructured to structured data types and how to classify data correctly.

topics.name: DataStorage
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn techniques to store data of all types for usage in an application.

topics.name: DataManipulation
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to interact with and manipulate data from storage providers using techniques such as SQL or SSH.

topics.name: DataWrangling
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn to convert data from one type to another using data wrangling techniques.

topics.name: ManagingState
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about effectively using different patterns to manage the state in an application.

topics.name: DesignFundamentals
topics.description: Learn to use basic design terminology, and have an understanding of human experience design as it relates to their work.

topics.name: IntroductiontoDesignThinking
topics.description: Build an understanding of each phase of the design thinking methodology, enough to help and support software teams in their problem-solving processes.

topics.name: DesignSystems
topics.description: Learn how to use, contribute to, and maintain a design system for building web applications.

topics.name: AccessibilityandUniversalDesignFundament
topics.description: Learn how to build software that meets the accessibility needs of its users.

topics.name: WorkingwithDigitalProductDesigners
topics.description: Learn how to collaborate with designers in the field by understanding the role they play in the creation of quality software.

topics.name: CloudProviders
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about cloud computing and the wide range of services an application can use with this topic.

topics.name: InfrastructureasCode
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to provision cloud services quickly and reliably using infrastructure as code techniques.

topics.name: BackingServices
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to work with the attached services required for an application to run.

topics.name: ManagingMultipleEnvironments
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to manage multiple environments such as dev, staging, and production for an application.

topics.name: WorkingwithCloudEngineers
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the role of a cloud engineer and how to work with them on your team effectively.

topics.name: HistoryoftheInternet
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the history of the internet and how connected computing changed humanity forever.

topics.name: InternetProtocols
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the TCP/IP protocol suite that powers the internet and how it practically applies to the software you create.

topics.name: NetworkConfiguration
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the basics of configuring networks, including setting up firewall rules, subnets, and assigning static IPs to machines.

topics.name: DomainsandDNS
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how the DNS system works and create DNS records connecting domains to web services.

topics.name: SSLCertificates
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the certificate authorities and how to encrypt traffic to web services using TLS/SSL certificates.

topics.name: CollectingLogsErrorsMetricsandTraces
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the different types of telemetry data that can be collected in an application.

topics.name: TheGoldenSignals
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about four valuable metrics to measure the health of an application.

topics.name: EffectiveAlerting
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about signal vs. noise and how to create valuable alerts that raise the alarm when something goes wrong in an application.

topics.name: Debugging
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about techniques to debug and resolve application issues using telemetry data.

topics.name: ImprovingPerformance
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to improve the scalability and reliability of an application based on its utilization.

topics.name: ManagingReleases
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about change management and how to release new versions of an application with as little downtime as possible.

topics.name: ManagingIncidents
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn to keep calm under the stress of resolving production application incidents.

topics.name: ContinuousIntegrationandDeployment
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn to automate the release process using continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD) pipelines.

topics.name: PerformanceTesting
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to verify the performance of an application using load tests, smoke tests, and black-box testing.

topics.name: WorkingwithSiteReliabilityEngineers
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the role of a site reliability engineer and how to work with them on your team effectively.

topics.name: ProductMarketFitPMF
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn the influence product-market fit has on the overarching mission of a business.

topics.name: StagesofaCompany
topics.description: With this topic you'll learn how to correlate the stages of a company to its journey toward product-market fit.

topics.name: ProductLifeCycleStages
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how a software engineer's focus may change as the goals of a product change.

topics.name: InterteamCoordination
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how most companies coordinate software development between their product teams and the surrounding business.

topics.name: IntrateamCoordination
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how most product teams organize their composition of skill sets within the team and how a software engineer can work specifically with a product manager on their team.

topics.name: TypesofLanguages
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the landscape of programming languages, their common features, and how to recognize them regardless of syntax.

topics.name: DeepLanguageLearning
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to gain fluency in a programming language and develop a deep understanding of its functionality and idiosyncrasies.

topics.name: PolyglotLearning
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to adapt your understanding of one programming language to gain proficiency in another.

topics.name: Dependencies
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about package management and the ecosystem of third-party dependencies for a programming language.

topics.name: PowerofPairProgramming
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to solve problems with other software engineers (and have fun while doing it) using pair programming techniques.

topics.name: OWASPTop10
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the most common application security issues and how to mitigate them.

topics.name: AuthenticationandAuthorization
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about identity providers and how to use OAuth 2.0 to manage authentication and authorization in an application.

topics.name: ManagingSecrets
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about application secrets and how to manage them effectively.

topics.name: ContinuousSecurity
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about methods to integrate security scanning into a project CI/CD pipeline.

topics.name: WorkingwithSecurityEngineers
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the role of a security engineer and how to work with them on your team effectively.

topics.name: EditorsandIDEs
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the landscape of editors, IDEs, and how to choose an editor and make it your own.

topics.name: CommandLine
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to use the command line (also known as the shell) and create scripts to make running complex commands easier.

topics.name: VersionControl
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about the importance of source control, Git commands, handling merge conflicts, popular branching strategies, and more.

topics.name: Productivity
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn about some productivity tools and how to estimate work effectively to set proper expectations with stakeholders.

topics.name: ManagingyourLocalEnvironment
topics.description: With this topic, you'll learn how to set up your local environment and streamline it to increase your productivity.

topics.name: PythonDataStructuresandAlgorithms
topics.description: A data structure is a named location that can be used to store and organize data. And, an algorithm is a collection of steps to solve a particular problem. Learning data structures and algorithms allow us to write efficient and optimized computer progr...

topics.name: PythonArraysandLinkedLists
topics.description: Arrays store elements in contiguous memory locations, resulting in easily calculable addresses for the elements stored and this allows faster access to an element at a specific index. Linked lists are less rigid in their storage structure and elements...

topics.name: PythonHeapsStacksandQueues
topics.description: **Stacks:** Operations are performed LIFO (last in, first out), which means that the last element added will be the first one removed. A stack can be implemented using an array or a linked list. If the stack runs out of memory, it’s called a stack over...

topics.name: PythonHashTables
topics.description: Hash Table, Map, HashMap, Dictionary or Associative are all the names of the same data structure. It is a data structure that implements a set abstract data type, a structure that can map keys to values.

topics.name: PythonBinarySearchTrees
topics.description: A binary search tree, also called an ordered or sorted binary tree, is a rooted binary tree data structure with the key of each internal node being greater than all the keys in the respective node’s left subtree and less than the ones in its right subtree

topics.name: PythonRecursion
topics.description: Recursion is a method of solving a computational problem where the solution depends on solutions to smaller instances of the same problem. Recursion solves such recursive problems by using functions that call themselves from within their own code.

topics.name: SortingAlgorithmsinPython
topics.description: Sorting refers to arranging data in a particular format. Sorting algorithm specifies the way to arrange data in a particular order. Most common orders are in numerical or lexicographical order.

topics.name: PythonIterators
topics.description: An iterator is an object that contains a countable number of values. An iterator is an object that can be iterated upon, meaning that you can traverse through all the values. Technically, in Python, an iterator is an object which implements the iterato...

topics.name: PythonRegularExpressions
topics.description: A regular expression is a sequence of characters that specifies a search pattern in text. Usually such patterns are used by string-searching algorithms for “find” or “find and replace” operations on strings, or for input validation.

topics.name: PythonDecorators
topics.description: decorator is a design pattern in Python that allows a user to add new functionality to an existing object without modifying its structure. Decorators are usually called before the definition of a function you want to decorate.

topics.name: PythonLambdas
topics.description: Python Lambda Functions are anonymous function means that the function is without a name. As we already know that the def keyword is used to define a normal function in Python. Similarly, the lambda keyword is used to define an anonymous function in Py...

topics.name: PythonListComprehensions
topics.description: List comprehensions are a concise way to create a list using a single line of code in Python. They are a powerful tool for creating and manipulating lists, and they can be used to simplify and shorten code.

topics.name: PythonGeneratorExpressions
topics.description: Generator expressions are a concise way to create a generator using a single line of code in Python. They are similar to list comprehensions, but instead of creating a list, they create a generator object that produces the values on-demand, as they are...
